name: AVM Autonomous Operations - Complete System Overview

parameters.content:
# AVM Autonomous Operations
## Complete AI Agent System - AnyVision Media

**27 autonomous workflows** across **9 departments** | **250+ nodes** | Orchestrated by Central AI

Built with: n8n Cloud, Claude Sonnet AI, Airtable, Supabase, Xero, Gmail, WhatsApp Business API, OpenRouter, SerpAPI, Google Workspace

---
Each section below contains the **full production workflow** for that department agent. Workflows execute autonomously on schedules or webhooks, with the Central Orchestrator monitoring health, routing events, and generating reports.

## CENTRAL ORCHESTRATOR

Coordinates all 6 department agents | Health monitoring, event routing, KPI aggregation, weekly reports

**4 workflows** in this department

### ORCH-01 Health Monitor
**12 nodes** | Trigger: Schedule

value.Title: =Agent {{ $json.agentId }} - {{ $json.status }}: {{ $json.totalErrors }} errors
value.Description: =Agent {{ $json.agentId }} has {{ $json.totalErrors }} errors across {{ $json.workflowsChecked }} workflows. Health score: {{ $json.healthScore }}. Error workflows: {{ JSON.stringify($json.errorWorkflows) }}

### Client Relations - Onboarding Automation (CR-03)
**8 nodes** | Trigger: Webhook

parameters.subject: =Welcome to AnyVision Media, {{ $json.client.name || 'there' }}!
parameters.message:
=

Welcome to AnyVision Media!

Hi {{ $json.client.name || 'there' }},

Welcome aboard! We're thrilled to have {{ $json.client.company || 'you' }} as part of the AnyVision Media family.

Getting Started in 3 Easy Steps:

  1. Log in to your portal: portal.anyvisionmedia.com
  2. Complete your profile: Add your business details and preferences
  3. Explore your dashboard: See real-time analytics and workflow status

Your Plan: {{ $json.client.plan }}

Here's what's included with your plan and how to make the most of it:

  • AI-powered workflow automation
  • Real-time performance dashboard
  • Dedicated support via email or WhatsApp

If you have any questions, simply reply to this email or reach out to us anytime.

Looking forward to building something amazing together!

Best regards,
Ian Immelman
AnyVision Media

### Client Relations - Satisfaction Pulse (CR-04)
**11 nodes** | Trigger: Schedule

parameters.jsonBody:
={ "model": "{{ $('System Config').first().json.aiModel }}", "max_tokens": 3000, "temperature": 0.7, "messages": [ { "role": "system", "content": "You are a client relations specialist for AnyVision Media, a digital media and AI automation agency based in South Africa. ## Your Task Draft a personalized check-in email for each client based on their usage data, subscription status, and recent activity. The email should feel genuine and personal, not templated. ## Company Context - Brand: AnyVision Media - Owner: Ian Immelman - Services: AI workflow automation, web development, social media management - Tone: Professional but warm, relationship-focused, value-driven ## For Each Client, Generate: 1. A personalized subject line referencing something specific to their usage 2. A warm greeting using their name 3. 1-2 sentences acknowledging their specific usage patterns or achievements 4. A helpful tip or suggestion based on their plan/usage 5. An open-ended question to encourage dialogue 6. A professional sign-off ## Output Format (JSON only, no markdown, no backticks): { "emails": [ { "client_email": "email", "client_name": "name", "subject": "personalized subject", "body_html": "

Full HTML email body

", "tone": "warm|celebratory|supportive|re-engaging" } ], "summary": "Brief summary of outreach batch" } ## Rules - Never use generic phrases like "Just checking in" or "Hope you're well" - Reference specific data points (e.g., "Your team ran 142 automations this month") - South African business context (ZAR, local references where natural) - Keep each email under 200 words - Flag any clients who seem disengaged for special attention" }, { "role": "user", "content": {{ JSON.stringify('CLIENT DATA: ' + JSON.stringify($json.clients).substring(0, 4000) + ' Total eligible clients: ' + $json.total_eligible) }} } ] }

parameters.content:
## CUSTOMER SUPPORT AGENT

AI ticket creation, SLA monitoring, auto-resolution & knowledge base building

**4 workflows** in this department

### Support Agent - Ticket Creator (SUP-01)
**11 nodes** | Trigger: Webhook

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "max_tokens": 800,
  "temperature": 0.3,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "system",
      "content": "You are a support ticket classifier for AnyVision Media, a digital media and AI automation agency in South Africa.

## Your Task
Classify the incoming support ticket: determine priority, department, and suggest a resolution.

## Priority Levels
- P1: Service down, security breach, data loss - immediate response needed
- P2: Major feature broken, billing error, client unable to work - response within 4h
- P3: Minor bug, feature request, general question - response within 8h
- P4: Nice-to-have, cosmetic issue, documentation - response within 24h

## Departments
- Accounting: Invoicing, billing, payments, Xero, subscriptions
- Marketing: Content, social media, campaigns, analytics
- Technical: Workflows, n8n, API errors, integrations, portal issues
- General: Other inquiries, feedback, partnerships

## Output Format (JSON only, no markdown, no backticks):
{
  "priority": "P1|P2|P3|P4",
  "department": "Accounting|Marketing|Technical|General",
  "category": "short category label",
  "ai_summary": "1-2 sentence summary of the issue",
  "suggested_resolution": "Suggested steps to resolve this issue",
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "reasoning": "Brief explanation of classification reasoning"
}

## Rules
- Be conservative with P1 - only true emergencies
- If unsure between two priorities, choose the higher one
- Include confidence score (0.0 to 1.0) reflecting how certain you are
- South African business context (ZAR, SAST timezone)"
    },
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": {{ JSON.stringify('SUBJECT: ' + $json.ticket.subject + '

BODY: ' + $json.ticket.body + '

CLIENT: ' + $json.ticket.email) }}
    }
  ]
}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({success: true, ticket_id: $('Create Ticket').first().json.fields ? $('Create Ticket').first().json.fields['Ticket ID'] : 'created', priority: $('Parse Classification').first().json.classification.priority, department: $('Parse Classification').first().json.classification.department, auto_resolve: $('Parse Classification').first().json.can_auto_resolve}) }}

parameters.content:
### Support Agent - Auto-Resolver (SUP-03)
**10 nodes** | Trigger: Schedule

parameters.jsonBody:
={ "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "max_tokens": 1200, "temperature": 0.4, "messages": [ { "role": "system", "content": "You are an AI support agent for AnyVision Media, a digital media and AI automation agency in South Africa. ## Your Task Using the provided Knowledge Base articles, draft a helpful response to the support ticket. Your response should resolve the issue or provide clear next steps. ## Response Guidelines 1. Be specific and actionable 2. Reference relevant KB articles where appropriate 3. Include step-by-step instructions when applicable 4. Maintain a professional but friendly tone 5. If the KB doesn't cover the issue, say so honestly ## Output Format (JSON only, no markdown, no backticks): { "response_html": "

Full HTML response to send to the client

", "confidence": 0.9, "kb_articles_used": ["Article ID 1", "Article ID 2"], "needs_human_review": false, "resolution_type": "full_resolution|partial_resolution|escalation_needed" } ## Rules - Keep responses under 300 words - Always sign off as "AnyVision Media Support Team" - Include a "Was this helpful?" prompt at the end - If confidence < 0.85, set needs_human_review to true" }, { "role": "user", "content": {{ JSON.stringify('TICKET SUBJECT: ' + $('Execute Workflow Trigger').first().json.subject + ' TICKET BODY: ' + $('Execute Workflow Trigger').first().json.body + ' CATEGORY: ' + $('Execute Workflow Trigger').first().json.category + ' KNOWLEDGE BASE ARTICLES: ' + JSON.stringify($input.all().map(r => ({title: (r.json.fields || r.json).Title || '', body: ((r.json.fields || r.json).Body || '').substring(0, 500), category: (r.json.fields || r.json).Category || ''})).slice(0, 10)).substring(0, 3000)) }} } ] }

parameters.content:
### Intelligence - Executive Report (INTEL-02)
**9 nodes** | Trigger: Schedule

parameters.jsCode:
// Format executive report as branded HTML email const summary = $json.choices[0].message.content; const data = $('Aggregate Data').first().json; // Build agent performance table rows const agentRows = Object.entries(data.agentTotals).map(([agent, t]) => { const name = agent.replace('agent_', '').replace(/_/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, l => l.toUpperCase()); return ` ${name} R${Math.round(t.revenueZar).toLocaleString()} ${Math.round(t.leadsGenerated)} ${Math.round(t.contentPublished)} ${t.avgSuccessRate}% `; }).join(''); const html = `

AVM Monthly Executive Report

${data.monthLabel}

R${Math.round(data.totalRevenue).toLocaleString()}
Revenue
${data.totalLeads}
Leads
${data.totalContent}
Content
${data.escalationCount}
Escalations

Agent Performance

${agentRows}
Agent Revenue Leads Content Success %

Executive Summary

${summary.replace(/\n/g, '
')}

Generated by AVM Intelligence Engine | INTEL-02 | ${data.kpiDataPoints} data points analyzed

`; return [{ json: { html, subject: 'AVM Monthly Executive Report - ' + data.monthLabel } }];

parameters.subject: ={{ $json.subject }}
parameters.message: ={{ $json.html }}
